title: phragmites communis subsp. humilis
intro: Phragmites communis subsp. humilis, also known as the lowland or dwarf common reed, is a perennial wetland grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Phragmites communis subsp. humilis include its ability to soothe coughs, reduce inflammation, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by drying and infusing the leaves and stems in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
